title: Local uv/python database MCP with a non-credential connection-string env resolves SAFE

origin.description: Single-server config from the public MCP catalog repo khopilot/amazing-mcp-for-productivity (ai_engineering/postgresql_mcp.json). A local 'uv run python -m mcp_database_tool' launcher with a single DB_CONNECTION_STRING env. The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained. The committed value is already upstream's own placeholder ('your-connection-string'); no real secret was present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim
no secret redaction needed: DB_CONNECTION_STRING value was already the upstream placeholder 'your-connection-string'

humanExpectation.rationale: A recognized local runtime ('uv run python -m mcp_database_tool', sourceKnown) with no broad path, no remote, no unpinned package (no npx/uvx package fetch — it runs a local module), and a single env key whose name (DB_CONNECTION_STRING) is NOT credential-shaped, so 'secrets.env-key' does not fire. With no blocking finding, the honest verdict is SAFE under current evidence (S1, read-only utility). This is the first real/redacted local-python SAFE case; previously real SAFE cases were docker-based (C011-C013, C024). It pins the true-negative that a connection-string env name is not treated as a secret key.
knownLimitations: secrets.env-key keys on credential-shaped substrings (TOKEN / SECRET / KEY / PASSWORD / CREDENTIAL / AUTH). DB_CONNECTION_STRING does not match, so it is correctly unflagged even though a real connection string can embed a password in its value. CallLint inspects config-key SHAPE, not values, by design; this is a documented limitation, not a missed secret in this placeholder case.

'uv run python -m <module>' is a recognized local runtime, so the source is observable and SAFE is reachable (same family as the RC-OBS-02 baseline C035). thisCaseMustNeverBeSafe is left false: this records the current contract, it does not assert SAFE is the only defensible answer.
